title: Add cancellation-fee tier tests
description: The cancellation-fee computation - booking.CancellationFee in internal/booking/policy.go - is a deliberate coverage gap: it has no tests at all, even though it decides how much a customer is charged. Write a table-driven test suite that covers the fee computation across all tiers of the default policy and at every boundary, so that any future change to the fee logic is caught.
acceptance_criteria: Table-driven tests for booking.CancellationFee exist in internal/booking using testify/assert.
A cancellation earlier than the policy cutoff is covered and expects a zero fee.
Every tier of the default cancellation policy is covered with a lead time inside that tier.
The exact cutoff boundary is covered: remaining lead time exactly equal to the cutoff.
Exact tier boundaries are covered: remaining lead time exactly equal to a tier's hours-before value.
A cancellation after the reservation start (negative lead time) is covered.
A policy with no tiers is covered.
A policy whose tiers are supplied in non-sorted order is covered.
go test ./... passes and no production (non-test) code is modified.
constraints: testify/assert only; no new test frameworks or assertion libraries.
Tests must be deterministic: fixed time values, no time.Now or sleeps.
Production code must not be changed; if the tests reveal what looks like a defect, report it instead of fixing it.
non_goals: Testing the HTTP cancel endpoint (covered elsewhere).

visible_validation.description: Diff adds test code only; no production file changed.

expected_artifacts: A new test file (or additions to an existing one) in internal/booking

human_review_rubric.criterion: All tiers and all boundary lead times of the default policy are exercised
Table-driven structure matches the existing test style
Each case's expected fee is justified by the documented policy semantics, not by copying the implementation's output
Review effort: cases are named clearly enough to audit in minutes

known_ambiguities: The expected behavior at exact boundaries (lead time equal to the cutoff; lead time equal to a tier's hours-before value) must be derived from the function's documented contract in internal/booking - state the reading you tested against.
Whether custom policies beyond the default need coverage is open; the default policy plus the no-tier and unsorted-tier cases is the minimum.
expected_failure_modes: Asserting whatever the implementation currently returns instead of the documented contract.
Missing the exact-boundary cases and covering only mid-tier lead times.
